reforms.description: Slovenia has implemented a series of reforms to enhance innovation, including generous R&D and investment tax allowances to stimulate private sector growth, the introduction of research vouchers to promote collaboration between companies and public research organisations, and the establishment of Centres of Excellence and Competencies to foster entrepreneurial dynamism. Additionally, spending on environment-related R&D has been increased to strengthen the link between public and business sectors, aiming to apply research outcomes to commercially viable solutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms are designed to enhance collaboration between the public and private sectors, stimulate R&D investment, and foster entrepreneurship, all of which are expected to contribute positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially reported as implemented in the OECD Economic Survey.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in the innovation landscape of Slovenia, particularly in enhancing collaboration and funding mechanisms, but do not constitute a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Despite recently increased spending on environment-related R&D, the link between public and business sector has to be strengthened to apply research outcomes to commercially viable solutions.
